dossiers.bear_case: Puerto Rico political/fiscal risk. FX exposure (LatAm currencies volatile). Macro slowdown. No imminent catalyst to force re-rating.
dossiers.bull_case: 13x PE vs 20x+ historical for payment processors. Puerto Rico economy recovery. LatAm digital payments secular growth. Dividend payer. Insider buying shows management sees undervaluation.
dossiers.catalyst: 5-insider accumulation cluster ($2.25M) May–June including multiple VP-level buys. Payment processor monopoly in Puerto Rico with growing LatAm footprint.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: No specific event this week. Steady compounder with insider conviction signal.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Priced for zero growth at 13x PE. Structural discount vs US peers reflects risk rather than business quality.

dossiers.bear_case: WTI dropped from $90 to $69 on Iran supply return. Doha talks TODAY could push oil lower. OPEC not offsetting Iran barrels. Revenue exposure to oil price.
dossiers.bull_case: 2-signal convergence (insider + smart money). 12.8x PE, 2.9% dividend. CEO buying personally. If oil stabilizes/rebounds, 30% upside to analyst $65 target. Midstream monetization optionality.
dossiers.catalyst: CEO + 5 insiders bought $681K May–June. Tweedy Browne position (smart money). Hidden midstream asset worth $1.2-1.5B. Woodford well results pending.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: US-Iran Doha talks June 30 (TODAY — oil supply risk). EIA inventory data Wednesday July 2. Woodford well results could pre-release anytime.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: At $69 WTI, MTDR is fairly valued. Today's Doha talks are a near-term overhang. Oil bounce or Woodford catalyst needed for re-rating.

dossiers.bear_case: Very small float (5.8M shares), wide bid-ask spread. Community banking facing NIM pressure as rates normalize. No catalyst to sustain breakout.
dossiers.bull_case: PE=12, 1.3% dividend. Insiders bought on the way up. At 52w high = price action confirming insider thesis. Low volume/float means small buying can move price. 2002-founded stable bank.
dossiers.catalyst: 4 insiders bought $366K May–June. Small CT community bank just hit 52-week high ($59.89 on June 29).
dossiers.catalyst_timing: No specific catalyst. Momentum at 52w high suggests breakout potential in thin holiday week.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: At 52w high — momentum already expressed. Hard to say what's still available.

dossiers.bear_case: Negative earnings (PE -18). Oil headwinds from Iran supply. 6-employee company = no operating leverage. High dividend may be unsustainable if production declines.
dossiers.bull_case: CEO + CFO buying. 9.7% dividend yield provides floor. Non-operated model = low capex, high margins on production. Potential oil bounce catalyst.
dossiers.catalyst: 5 insiders (CEO, CFO, directors) bought $839K May–June. Non-operated E&P royalty model with 9.7% dividend yield.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Oil price this week (Doha talks, EIA inventory). No specific company catalyst.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Dividend yield implies market skepticism. Oil-price dependency makes this a macro bet more than a company-specific play.

dossiers.bear_case: Securities fraud class action. ERP disaster still losing shelf space. Near-zero forward earnings with cash burn -$437M. 85% of the decline is justified by fundamentals. 10 insiders only bought $417K total = not huge conviction.
dossiers.bull_case: Extreme short-squeeze setup: 47% SI + 10 insider buys + holiday thin volume. If ANY positive news hits (shelf space recovery, ERP fix, analyst upgrade), forced covering could drive 15-30%+ in a single session. Pasture-raised eggs/butter are secular premium trends. Brand loyal customers.
dossiers.catalyst: 10 insiders bought in tight 7-day cluster (May 13-19). 47% short interest with 5+ days to cover. 79% crash from $53 high. Stock now at $11.30 with 10.8x trailing PE.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: No specific catalyst this week. Holiday-shortened week with thin volume = amplified moves. Q2 earnings August 6. Short sellers must cover at some point.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Most bad news IS priced in at $11 (10.8x trailing PE, 79% off highs). But forward earnings near zero means the trailing PE is misleading. Insiders betting on recovery; shorts betting on continued deterioration.

dossiers.bear_case: Cash burn -$437M annually. $5.5B total debt on $5.1B market cap. Near-zero earnings (PE 5389). Construction industry cyclicality. Take-private language is 7 days old — founders may simply be posturing.
dossiers.bull_case: 2-signal convergence (insider + activist). Founders are the activists — they control the outcome. $2.87M personal open-market buying shows conviction. Stock at $20 vs IPO reference $24.50 and analyst targets $39. If take-private announced, immediate 20-50% premium.
dossiers.catalyst: Founders Jabbok + William Schlacks bought $2.87M open-market May–June. Activist 13D filed with explicit take-private language. Founders control 81% voting power. Analyst consensus $39 (92% upside). Q1 revenue +38% YoY.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: No specific date — take-private at discretion of founders who control 81% of votes. Nexus Capital ($5B AUM) joined board. Any board meeting or filing could accelerate.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: 13D only 1 week old. Stock has not re-rated to reflect take-private probability. Significant premium still available if deal materializes.

dossiers.bear_case: Heavy debt load. Ring/SimpliSafe/Vivint competition eroding market share. Customer churn elevated. Insider buys were in May before stock dropped further to June 25 low.
dossiers.bull_case: PE=9x, 3.4% dividend. At absolute lows. 5 insiders bought before the low was set. Google partnership provides long-term monetization potential. Smart home market still growing.
dossiers.catalyst: 5 insiders bought $477K in May. Stock at 52-week low ($6.24 on June 25, now $6.50).
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Bounce from 52w low. Possible Google partnership news (existing $1.5B+ strategic investment). No specific event this week.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: At 52w low = maximum pessimism. But business is genuinely challenged. The bounce from $6.24 to $6.50 may be noise not signal.

dossiers.bear_case: No near-term catalyst. Insurance sector facing social inflation in casualty lines. No earnings announcement this week.
dossiers.bull_case: CEO Craig Kliethermes bought personally. Specialty insurance = pricing power. PE=13.5, solid dividend track record. 20% below 52w high suggests value.
dossiers.catalyst: CEO + 3 senior insiders bought $859K May–June. High-quality specialty insurer.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: No specific catalyst this week. Q2 results TBD. CEO buying typically precedes strong earnings.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Trading at a fair price for a quality insurer. CEO buying suggests he sees material undervaluation.

dossiers.bear_case: Only founded 2024 — no earnings history. Mortgage rate sensitivity. Very new business model with limited track record. Dividend sustainability uncertain.
dossiers.bull_case: $6.6M insider purchases = highest conviction signal by dollars. Novel Homesite Option Purchase Platform reduces capital intensity for homebuilders. Spun from Lennar (2025), inherits their land relationships. 9.5% dividend yield. PE=11. Ex-div July 6 creates mechanical buying pressure from dividend-capture funds this week.
dossiers.catalyst: 4 insiders bought $6.62M (LARGEST dollar amount of all finalists) May 11-18. Ex-dividend date July 6 creates dividend-capture buying pressure this week ($0.77/share, 9.5% yield).
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Ex-dividend date July 6 (next Monday) — buyers this week capture the $0.77 dividend. Homebuilder land data July 2-3. New company with limited analyst coverage.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: New company with minimal analyst coverage. Market has not yet fully valued the land-banking model. Ex-div pressure should drive price action this week.

dossiers.bear_case: Only $149K across 6 insiders = weak conviction per person ($25K each). Stock 48% below 52w high. Market may be pricing in structural pricing decline as equipment upgrades reduce demand for older refrigerants.
dossiers.bull_case: Summer is peak season for refrigerant demand. AIM Act reducing supply of legacy HFCs supports pricing power. PE=18. CEO + CFO + 4 others bought.
dossiers.catalyst: 6 insiders bought $149K May 13-14. Refrigerant distributor with AIM Act tailwind.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Peak summer HVAC season (June-August). EPA AIM Act HFC phase-down is structural. No specific this-week event.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: At 48% below 52w high, market is very skeptical about AIM Act benefits materializing. No near-term catalyst.
